_meta.title: Nestudio Production Pack V1 — Golden Nest (Living Room · Study/Office · Focus Detail)
_meta.note: Metadata + generation-prompt planning only (M9). No final images are produced by this file. Every entry is authored to the V2 Nest data contract in lib/nest-types.ts and the locked camera/DNA. M9.1 (contract alignment): the 4 slot types below are now LIVE in NestSlotType, cover/photo skins are standalone `surface` assets, and the 2 new interactions live in lib/fixtures/production-pack-v1.ts.

asset.name: display name → NestAsset.name
asset.surfaceKind: set only when category==='surface': which editable-surface kind this standalone skin fills (cover|photo). Maps to NestAsset.surfaceKind (M9.1).
asset.editableSurfaces: [{ id, label, kind, bounds, contentType, aspect }] — maps to NestAsset.editableSurfaces (M9.1); host assets declare their customizable regions
asset.transparentPng: { required, master, delivery, maxKB, alpha } — object PNG requirement
asset.visualBounds: { aspect 'w:h', footprintWidthNorm, footprintHeightNorm, baseAnchor{x,y} } — footprint on the 3:4 scene, normalized 0..1; baseAnchor is the snap point (bottom-centre for floor)
asset.zIndexGuidance: { suggestedZ, range:[min,max], note } — paint order guidance for the slot that hosts it
asset.compatibleSlotTypes: slot types this asset may snap into (all live in NestSlotType as of M9.1). Standalone `surface` assets are not slot-placed and use [].
asset.hotspotRegions: [{ id, label, bounds{x,y,width,height}, action }] — tappable sub-regions (mostly used when focused)
asset.editableSurfaces: [{ id, label, kind, bounds, contentType, aspect }] — creator-customizable surfaces (screen/photo/cover/note-board)
asset.projectionBehaviour: { isProjectionParent, isProjectable, projectionTargets[], accepts[] } — parent hosts child assets on a surface; child projects onto a parent
asset.focusSuitability: { focusable, focusScene, childCapacity } — whether tapping zooms into an editable Focus detail scene
asset.interaction: { defaultInteractionId, trigger, animation, contentType } → resolves to an Interaction in the library; null for pure ambient decor
asset.contractMapping: note on how this maps onto NestAsset / SceneSlot

background.name: NestTemplate.name
background.transparency: false — backgrounds are opaque baked stages
background.supportsSlots: which slot layout this stage leaves clean, well-lit room for
background.ambienceReady: keeps enough value range for engine-composited plum contact shadow + ambience tint

addedSlotTypes._note: APPROVED + ADDED in M9.1 — these are now LIVE in NestSlotType / NEST_SLOT_TYPES (lib/nest-types.ts). The taxonomy is 14 types. Assets mapped to these compose against the live contract.
addedSlotTypes.seat: sofas, armchairs, office/desk chairs (floor plane). 6 assets.
addedSlotTypes.table: coffee tables, side tables — surface furniture that is not a work desk (floor plane, projection parent). 3 assets.
addedSlotTypes.rug: floor coverings (floor plane, large footprint, lowest z, no contact shadow). 2 assets.
addedSlotTypes.pinboard: wall-mounted note/cork boards that host editable note children (front_wall plane, projection parent). 2 assets.

addedInteractions._note: APPROVED + ADDED in M9.1 — defined in lib/fixtures/production-pack-v1.ts (PRODUCTION_PACK_INTERACTIONS). Existing NestAnimation/NestContentType enums already support them; no type change was needed.

surfaceModel._note: APPROVED in M9.1 — cover/photo skins are STANDALONE surface assets (NestAsset with category='surface' + surfaceKind), NOT NestAssetVariants. Materialized in lib/fixtures/production-pack-v1.ts (PRODUCTION_PACK_SURFACE_ASSETS).

objectsTransparentPng.trueAlpha: real per-pixel alpha channel; no baked white/gray/solid background
objectsTransparentPng.noCheckerboard: no baked transparency checkerboard pattern in the raster
objectsTransparentPng.noBakedBackground: no room, floor, wall, or cast environment baked behind the object
objectsTransparentPng.noBakedFloorShadow: objects ship with NO contact/floor shadow — the engine composites the plum contact ellipse (visual-dna §12)
objectsTransparentPng.selfShadowOnly: baked self-shading must agree with upper-left warm key light
objectsTransparentPng.isolation: one object, centered, uncropped, fully in frame
objectsTransparentPng.aspect: transparent PNG master 1024×1024; delivery WebP ≤ 400 KB (visual-dna §29)
objectsTransparentPng.aspectMatchesVisualBounds: the cut-out's pixel aspect must match visualBounds.aspect so object-contain fills the slot box with no letterbox (golden-nest-v2 layout rule)

backgrounds.aspect: 3:4 portrait primary; master 1080×1440 (1440×1920 ok); delivery WebP ≤ 350 KB
backgrounds.noBakedFurniture: the stage is EMPTY — no furniture/props/people baked in (visual-dna §16, §28)
backgrounds.sceneBoxAlignment: the baked floor seam must sit in band y≈0.58–0.64 and match the declared sceneBox.floorSeamY
backgrounds.cleanZones: leaves clean, well-lit room for the declared supportsSlots layout; floor band low-busy with value range for plum contact shadow

shared.camera: front-facing cinematic, eye-level, ~5–10° downward tilt; NOT isometric/top-down/~30°/one-point tunnel
shared.dna: warm matte, rounded, one accent, warm-light #fff6e0 / cool-plum-shadow #46365a, upper-left key
shared.noText: no baked text/watermark/logo/UI unless the asset is explicitly a text surface (e.g. a book spine label) and the prompt requests it
shared.mobileReadability: silhouette + key detail must read at 64px and 128px
shared.hotspotSurfaceAlignment: editable surfaces + hotspot regions must align to the declared bounds in the final raster

focus-detail.name: Focus Detail Pack
focus-detail.note: Child / projected objects + editable-surface skins that appear inside a parent's Focus detail scene (TV console, bookshelf, desk surface, pinboard). Most are projectable (not scene-slot placed); the surface skins re-texture a parent's editable surface. All inherit interactions from their host per the resolveInteractionId precedence (assignment → asset → slot).
